name: Avocado Toast with Egg
description: Crisp toasted sourdough topped with creamy mashed avocado and a perfectly fried egg — a simple breakfast that never gets old.
steps: Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
Mash the avocado in a bowl with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Fry the eggs to your liking in a lightly oiled pan.
Spread the mashed avocado onto the toast and top with the fried egg.
Finish with a pinch of chili flakes if desired.

name: Greek Salad
description: A refreshing no-cook salad of crisp cucumber, juicy tomatoes, olives, and salty feta tossed in olive oil.
steps: Chop the tomatoes and cucumber into bite-sized chunks.
Thinly slice the red onion.
Combine tomatoes, cucumber, onion, feta, and olives in a bowl.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with oregano.
Toss gently and serve immediately.

name: Margherita Flatbread
description: A quick flatbread pizza topped with tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and basil — ready faster than delivery.
steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (425°F).
Spread tomato sauce evenly over each flatbread.
Top with slices of fresh mozzarella.
Bake for 8-10 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Top with fresh basil, a drizzle of olive oil, and a pinch of salt.

name: Veggie Stir Fry
description: A colorful medley of crisp vegetables tossed in a savory garlic-soy sauce, on the table in one pan.
steps: Heat sesame oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add garlic and ginger, stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the vegetables and stir fry for 5-7 minutes until crisp-tender.
Pour in the soy sauce and toss to coat evenly.
Serve hot over rice or noodles.

name: Classic Cheese Omelette
description: Fluffy folded eggs with melted cheese — a fast, protein-packed classic for any time of day.
steps: Whisk the eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a nonstick pan over medium heat.
Pour in the eggs and let set for 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle cheese over half the omelette and fold over.
Cook for another minute, then slide onto a plate and garnish with chives.

name: Caprese Salad
description: Simple Italian elegance: layered tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, and basil finished with balsamic glaze.
steps: Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a plate.
Tuck basil leaves between the slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic glaze.
Season with salt and pepper before serving.

name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
description: Juicy shrimp seared fast in a rich garlic butter sauce — restaurant flavor in barely 10 minutes.
steps: Melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add shrimp in a single layer and cook 2 minutes per side until pink.
Stir in lemon juice and parsley.
Season and serve immediately with crusty bread.

name: Chicken Quesadilla
description: Crispy tortillas stuffed with melty cheese and seasoned chicken, sliced into wedges for easy sharing.
ingredients: 1 cup shredded cheddar or Monterey Jack
steps: Toss the shredded chicken with cumin and bell pepper.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Place a tortilla in the pan, sprinkle half with cheese and chicken mixture, then fold over.
Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden and the cheese melts.
Slice into wedges and serve with salsa or sour cream.

name: Tomato Basil Soup
description: A warm, velvety soup made from simmered tomatoes and fresh basil — comfort food in a bowl.
steps: Heat olive oil in a pot and sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add crushed tomatoes and vegetable stock, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend until smooth using an immersion blender.
Stir in the cream and torn basil leaves.
Season to taste and serve warm.

name: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A humble Italian classic: spaghetti tossed in garlicky, chili-flecked olive oil with a touch of parsley.
steps: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
While pasta cooks, gently heat olive oil with garlic and chili flakes until golden and fragrant.
Toss the drained spaghetti into the garlic oil, adding a splash of pasta water to loosen.
Stir in parsley and season with salt.
Top with parmesan if desired and serve immediately.

name: Buddha Bowl
description: A vibrant, balanced bowl of grains, roasted veggies, and greens finished with a creamy tahini drizzle.
steps: Arrange quinoa, roasted sweet potato, chickpeas, and spinach in a bowl.
Top with sliced avocado.
Whisk together tahini and lemon juice with a splash of water until smooth.
Drizzle the dressing over the bowl.
Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
description: Buttery, golden bread with a gooey, melty cheese center — the ultimate quick comfort food.
steps: Butter one side of each bread slice.
Place two slices butter-side down in a pan, add cheese, and top with the remaining bread, butter-side up.
Cook over medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and the cheese melts.
Slice and serve hot.

name: Beef Tacos
description: Seasoned ground beef piled into warm tortillas with fresh toppings — a weeknight favorite.
steps: Brown the ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add taco seasoning and a splash of water, simmer for 5 minutes.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave.
Fill each tortilla with beef, lettuce, tomato, and cheese.
Top with sour cream and serve immediately.

name: Fluffy Pancakes
description: Soft, fluffy pancakes stacked high and finished with syrup — a weekend breakfast staple.
steps: Whisk together flour, baking powder, and sugar in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, whisk milk, egg, and melted butter.
Combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed (a few lumps are fine).
Pour batter onto a hot, lightly greased griddle and cook until bubbles form, then flip.
Cook until golden on both sides and serve with maple syrup.

name: Hummus & Veggie Wrap
description: A fresh, no-cook wrap loaded with creamy hummus and crunchy vegetables — perfect for a fast lunch.
steps: Spread a generous layer of hummus over each tortilla.
Layer the carrot, spinach, cucumber, and cabbage down the center.
Fold in the sides and roll up tightly.
Slice in half and serve immediately.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice
description: A quick way to use up leftover rice, tossed with crisp vegetables, egg, and soy sauce.
steps: Heat oil in a large pan or wok over high heat.
Add garlic and frozen vegetables, stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
Push to one side, pour in the eggs, and scramble until just set.
Add the rice and soy sauce, tossing everything together until heated through.
Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: Turkey Club Panini
description: A pressed sandwich with layers of turkey, cheese, and crisp bacon — hearty and satisfying.
steps: Spread mayonnaise on one side of two bread slices.
Layer turkey, cheese, and bacon between the bread, mayo-side in.
Butter the outside of the sandwiches.
Grill in a panini press or skillet until golden and the cheese melts, about 4 minutes.
Slice diagonally and serve warm.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs gently poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a one-pan Middle Eastern classic.
steps: Sauté onion and bell pepper in olive oil until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, and paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Make small wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook until the egg whites are set, about 5-6 minutes. Garnish with parsley.

name: Berry Smoothie Bowl
description: A thick, spoonable smoothie topped with fresh fruit and granola — breakfast that feels like dessert.
steps: Blend the frozen berries, banana, yogurt, and milk until thick and smooth.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola and fresh fruit.
Serve immediately with a spoon.

name: Quick Chickpea Curry
description: A warmly spiced, creamy curry made from pantry-staple chickpeas — comforting and fast enough for a weeknight.
steps: Heat oil in a pot and sauté onion until soft.
Add garlic, curry powder, and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in chickpeas and coconut milk.
Simmer for 12-15 minutes until slightly thickened.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve over rice.

name: Caesar Salad
description: Crisp romaine tossed in a tangy, garlicky dressing with parmesan and crunchy croutons.
steps: Wash and chop the romaine lettuce.
Toss the lettuce with Caesar dressing until evenly coated.
Add croutons and half the parmesan, tossing gently.
Top with remaining parmesan and a crack of black pepper.
Serve immediately while the croutons are still crisp.

name: BLT Sandwich
description: The classic combo of crispy bacon, lettuce, and tomato between toasted bread with mayo.
steps: Cook the bacon in a skillet until crisp, about 5-6 minutes.
Toast the bread slices.
Spread mayonnaise on each slice of toast.
Layer lettuce, tomato, and bacon between the slices.
Cut in half and serve immediately.

name: Egg Fried Noodles
description: Quick wok-fried noodles tossed with scrambled egg, soy sauce, and crisp vegetables.
steps: Heat oil in a wok over high heat and scramble the eggs, then set aside.
Add cabbage and carrot to the wok, stir frying for 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked noodles and soy sauce, tossing to combine.
Return the scrambled egg to the wok and mix everything together.
Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: Bruschetta
description: Toasted baguette slices topped with a fresh mix of tomato, basil, and garlic.
steps: Toast the baguette slices until golden.
Rub each slice with a cut clove of garlic while still warm.
Combine diced tomatoes, basil, olive oil, and salt in a bowl.
Spoon the tomato mixture generously onto each toasted slice.
Serve immediately.

name: Tuna Melt
description: A warm, open-faced sandwich of creamy tuna salad topped with melted cheese.
steps: Mix the tuna with mayonnaise, celery, salt, and pepper.
Spread the tuna mixture evenly onto each slice of bread.
Top each slice with a slice of cheese.
Broil in the oven for 3-4 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Serve warm.

name: Vegetable Quesadilla
description: Crispy tortillas filled with melted cheese and colorful sautéed vegetables.
steps: Sauté the bell pepper, corn, and onion in oil until softened, about 4 minutes.
Heat a separate skillet over medium heat.
Place a tortilla in the pan, sprinkle with cheese and the vegetable mixture, then fold over.
Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden and the cheese melts.
Slice into wedges and serve with salsa.

name: Miso Soup
description: A light, savory Japanese soup with tofu, seaweed, and a delicate miso broth.
steps: Heat the dashi or stock in a pot until warm but not boiling.
Whisk the miso paste with a ladle of warm stock until dissolved, then stir back into the pot.
Add the tofu and wakame, warming through for 2-3 minutes.
Avoid boiling once the miso is added, as it can dull the flavor.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with spring onions.

name: Falafel Wrap
description: Crispy falafel wrapped up with fresh salad and a creamy tahini sauce.
steps: Warm or fry the falafel according to package instructions, about 8-10 minutes.
Warm the flatbreads in a dry pan.
Layer lettuce, tomato, and falafel onto each flatbread.
Drizzle generously with tahini sauce.
Fold or roll up and serve immediately.

name: Caprese Pasta Salad
description: A cold pasta salad combining cherry tomatoes, mozzarella pearls, and basil in a light dressing.
steps: Cook the pasta according to package instructions, then drain and cool slightly.
Combine the pasta with cherry tomatoes, mozzarella, and basil in a large bowl.
Whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the pasta salad and toss to coat.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.

name: Chicken Caesar Wrap
description: Grilled chicken, crisp romaine, and Caesar dressing rolled up into a satisfying wrap.
steps: Toss the romaine with Caesar dressing and parmesan in a bowl.
Warm the tortillas slightly in a dry pan.
Layer the chicken and dressed romaine mixture onto each tortilla.
Fold in the sides and roll up tightly.
Slice in half and serve immediately.

name: Baked Beans on Toast
description: A simple, comforting classic — warm baked beans spooned over buttery toast.
steps: Heat the baked beans in a small pot over medium heat until warmed through.
Toast the bread slices until golden.
Butter the toast generously.
Spoon the warm beans over the toast.
Top with grated cheese if desired and serve immediately.

name: Peanut Butter Banana Toast
description: A quick, energizing snack of creamy peanut butter and sliced banana on warm toast.
steps: Toast the bread slices until golden.
Spread peanut butter evenly over each slice.
Arrange banana slices on top.
Drizzle with honey and dust with cinnamon if desired.
Serve immediately.

name: Cobb Salad
description: A hearty composed salad with chicken, egg, bacon, avocado, and blue cheese in neat rows.
steps: Arrange the lettuce as a bed on a large plate or bowl.
Arrange chicken, egg, bacon, avocado, and blue cheese in rows on top.
Drizzle with vinaigrette dressing just before serving.
Toss together at the table, or serve composed for presentation.

name: Margherita Panini
description: A pressed sandwich version of the classic Margherita, with melty mozzarella and fresh basil.
steps: Layer mozzarella, tomato, and basil between two slices of bread.
Brush the outside of the sandwiches with olive oil.
Grill in a panini press or skillet until golden and the cheese melts, about 4 minutes.
Slice in half and serve warm.

name: Sweetcorn Fritters
description: Crispy, golden fritters packed with sweet corn kernels — great as a snack or light meal.
steps: Combine the corn, flour, eggs, spring onions, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Spoon the batter into the pan in small rounds.
Fry for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crisp.
Drain on paper towel and serve warm.

name: Quick Chili con Carne
description: A hearty, spiced ground beef and bean chili, ready well before dinnertime rush.
steps: Brown the ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add onion and garlic, cooking until softened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in chili powder and cumin, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the crushed tomatoes and kidney beans, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Season to taste and serve hot, with rice or bread.

name: Shrimp Tacos
description: Zesty, spiced shrimp tucked into warm tortillas with a crisp cabbage slaw.
steps: Toss the shrimp with chili powder and cumin.
Cook the shrimp in a hot skillet for 2 minutes per side until pink and cooked through.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or microwave.
Mix the shredded cabbage with sour cream and a squeeze of lime juice.
Fill each tortilla with shrimp and slaw, then serve with extra lime.
